name: AETHERUS Intelligence Layer v0 Scenarios
source_section: Fixture library, pipelines, global output contract, safety policy, error handling
boundary_copy: Deterministic prototype trace. Static browser-side evaluation. Not live AI execution. Not a production audit ledger.

scenarios.title: Valid Release Eligibility
scenarios.summary: A bounded job ticket progresses through static gates and becomes eligible for illustrative release.

verdict.reason: All configured static gate expectations pass for this illustrative fixture.

release_eligibility.label: Eligible in prototype trace
release_eligibility.explanation: The scenario has complete receipts, no configured blocking faults, and a pass verdict. This is an illustrative eligibility explanation, not an operational release decision.

scenarios.static_retry_policy: No runtime retry loop is executed. Retry concepts are represented only as static policy language.

gate_results.note: No cache-hit branch selected; proceed signal represented.
Execution-plan metadata is accepted for this fixture.
Canonical job-ticket format is represented as valid.
Constraints packet metadata is represented as valid.
No critical forbidden pattern is selected.
Origin metadata is release-eligible in this illustrative trace.

events.note: Fixture input is framed as a bounded job ticket.

decision_explanation.why_verdict: The scenario passes because all configured static gate expectations are satisfied and no blocking fault is selected by the fixture.
decision_explanation.determining_condition: Complete receipts, pass gate results, and no selected safety or schema failure.
decision_explanation.different_verdict_requires: A selected schema fault, missing receipt, critical safety pattern, or failed gate fixture.

decisive_gate.label: Schema Validation
decisive_gate.reason: The fixture reaches the final validation boundary with complete receipts and no configured blocking fault.
decisive_gate.prevents_or_permits: Permits prototype release eligibility when the static fixture has complete receipts and no blocking validation fault.

active_layer_explanation.reason: The trace focuses on the audit and release boundary because the decisive question is whether the completed fixture can be treated as release-eligible in the prototype model.
active_layer_explanation.related_evidence_mapping: Maps to artefacts tagged with AUDIT_RELEASE and release eligibility evidence metadata.

operational_evidence_required: Real input/output artifact would be required for operational evidence.
Persistent trace storage would be required for operational evidence.
Append-only ledger semantics would be required for operational evidence.
Hash-chain verification would be required for operational evidence.
Backend validation would be required for operational evidence.
Authenticated actor identity would be required for operational evidence.
Reproducible execution logs would be required for operational evidence.
Test result artifact would be required for operational evidence.
Security review would be required for operational evidence.
Storage/audit retention model would be required for operational evidence.

assertions.expected_boundary_note: Illustrative eligibility only; not an operational release decision.

scenarios.title: Critical Safety Freeze
scenarios.summary: A GF04 wrapper pattern triggers the critical safety scan and activates the Freeze chamber.
scenarios.source_section: Fixture library: TC-SAFETY-001; safety policy; VAL-SAFE-001

verdict.reason: Critical forbidden pattern GF04 is selected in the fixture.

state_chamber.label: Freeze
state_chamber.action: Halt propagation in the illustrative trace

release_eligibility.label: Not eligible
release_eligibility.explanation: Critical pattern detection forces a fail verdict and freeze state. This is a deterministic prototype explanation, not live safety enforcement.

scenarios.static_retry_policy: No runtime retry loop is executed. Critical safety failure terminates this illustrative trace.

gate_results.note: Derived AETHERUS interface mapping from fail verdict to Freeze chamber.

events.note: GF04 wrapper condition is selected by the fixture.

decision_explanation.why_verdict: The scenario fails because the fixture selects GF04, a critical forbidden wrapper pattern.
decision_explanation.determining_condition: A non-excludable critical safety pattern is present in the static fixture.
decision_explanation.different_verdict_requires: Removal of the selected GF04 condition and a pass through the safety scan.

decisive_gate.label: Critical Safety Scan
decisive_gate.reason: GF04 is selected by the fixture and is marked critical and non-excludable in the manifest model.
decisive_gate.prevents_or_permits: Prevents propagation when a critical forbidden pattern is selected by the static fixture.

active_layer_explanation.reason: The trace focuses on the recovery path because the decisive safety failure routes the fixture into the Freeze chamber.
active_layer_explanation.related_evidence_mapping: Maps to artefacts tagged with RECOVERY_PATH, freeze semantics, and failure-handling evidence.

operational_evidence_required: Real contaminated artifact would be required for operational evidence.
Raw input/output capture would be required for operational evidence.
Backend validator implementation would be required for operational evidence.
Reproducible safety-scan logs would be required for operational evidence.
Persistent trace storage would be required for operational evidence.
Authenticated reviewer identity would be required for operational evidence.
Security review would be required for operational evidence.
Test result artifact would be required for operational evidence.

assertions.expected_boundary_note: Static safety fixture only; not live safety enforcement.

scenarios.title: Missing Constraints Escalation
scenarios.summary: A missing constraints packet creates an incomplete handoff receipt and escalation state.
scenarios.source_section: Fixture library: TC-SCHEMA-001; error handling E_MISSING_INPUT

verdict.reason: Required constraints_packet input is absent in this fixture.

state_chamber.label: Escalate
state_chamber.action: Raise missing-input condition for human review

release_eligibility.label: Blocked by missing input
release_eligibility.explanation: The scenario cannot proceed because a required input is missing. v0 reports the boundary as an illustrative escalation trace.

scenarios.static_retry_policy: No runtime retry loop is executed. External injection is represented only as static policy language.

gate_results.note: Derived AETHERUS interface mapping from escalate verdict to Escalate chamber.

events.note: Missing constraints packet produces incomplete receipt.

decision_explanation.why_verdict: The scenario escalates because the required constraints_packet is absent in the fixture.
decision_explanation.determining_condition: The input contract cannot be satisfied because a required handoff item is missing.
decision_explanation.different_verdict_requires: A present and valid constraints packet.

decisive_gate.label: Input Contract Validation
decisive_gate.reason: The fixture marks constraints_packet as missing, so the input contract boundary cannot pass.
decisive_gate.prevents_or_permits: Prevents progression when required input material is absent and permits only an illustrative escalation state.

active_layer_explanation.reason: The trace focuses on the recovery path because the missing input condition routes the fixture into Escalate rather than release flow.
active_layer_explanation.related_evidence_mapping: Maps to artefacts tagged with RECOVERY_PATH and human escalation evidence.

operational_evidence_required: Real job ticket would be required for operational evidence.
Real constraints packet would be required for operational evidence.
Backend input validator would be required for operational evidence.
Authenticated actor identity would be required for operational evidence.
Reproducible missing-input logs would be required for operational evidence.
Persistent trace storage would be required for operational evidence.
Escalation workflow record would be required for operational evidence.
Security review would be required for operational evidence.

assertions.expected_boundary_note: Static missing-input fixture only; not backend orchestration.

scenarios.title: Adapter Failure Escalation
scenarios.summary: A malformed job-ticket normalization path triggers the adapter failure boundary.

verdict.reason: Adapter normalization is selected as failed for this fixture.

release_eligibility.label: Blocked by adapter boundary
release_eligibility.explanation: The scenario exits at the normalization gate. This is static adapter-boundary evidence, not a live parser or model execution.

scenarios.static_retry_policy: No runtime retry loop is executed. Adapter failure exits as a static escalation trace.

decision_explanation.why_verdict: The scenario escalates because adapter normalization is selected as failed for this fixture.
decision_explanation.determining_condition: The static fixture marks the G1A normalization boundary as unsuccessful.
decision_explanation.different_verdict_requires: Valid normalization from ingress payload to canonical job-ticket representation.

decisive_gate.label: Adapter Gate
decisive_gate.reason: The fixture selects the adapter failure branch before downstream constraints or release evaluation can proceed.
decisive_gate.prevents_or_permits: Prevents progression beyond normalization when the static adapter boundary is failed.

active_layer_explanation.reason: The trace focuses on Governance Gates because the decisive condition is a gate-level adapter failure.
active_layer_explanation.related_evidence_mapping: Maps to artefacts tagged with GATE_DECISION, G1A, and gate validation evidence.

operational_evidence_required: Real ingress payload would be required for operational evidence.
Adapter implementation would be required for operational evidence.
Backend validation logs would be required for operational evidence.
Reproducible parser/normalization test result would be required for operational evidence.
Model/API boundary documentation would be required if model output is involved.
Authenticated actor identity would be required for operational evidence.
Persistent trace storage would be required for operational evidence.
Security review would be required for operational evidence.

assertions.expected_boundary_note: Static adapter-boundary fixture only; not a live parser or orchestration service.

scenarios.title: Idempotency Cache Hit
scenarios.summary: The idempotency gate returns a bounded cache-hit verdict and skips downstream steps in the illustrative trace.
scenarios.source_section: Pipelines: step 0; event ledger contract RES-024

verdict.reason: Static fixture selects a bounded cache-hit branch.

release_eligibility.label: Eligible by static cache-hit trace
release_eligibility.explanation: The scenario demonstrates idempotency semantics with an illustrative in-memory trace event. It does not retrieve persisted artifacts.

scenarios.static_retry_policy: No runtime retry loop is executed. Downstream skip is represented only as static control-flow explanation.

events.note: Static trace demonstrates cache-hit control semantics without persistence.

decision_explanation.why_verdict: The scenario passes because the fixture selects a bounded cache-hit branch at the idempotency gate.
decision_explanation.determining_condition: The static fixture marks G_IDEMPOTENCY as a cache hit before downstream gates are evaluated.
decision_explanation.different_verdict_requires: No cache-hit fixture condition and continuation into downstream gates.

decisive_gate.label: Idempotency Gate
decisive_gate.reason: The fixture selects a cache-hit branch at the authority/idempotency boundary.
decisive_gate.prevents_or_permits: Permits an illustrative pass/cache-hit outcome while skipping downstream steps as static control-flow explanation.

active_layer_explanation.reason: The trace focuses on Authority because idempotency is evaluated before downstream execution in the static pipeline model.
active_layer_explanation.related_evidence_mapping: Maps to artefacts tagged with AUTHORITY_CHECK and G_IDEM evidence metadata.

operational_evidence_required: Real input hash would be required for operational evidence.
Real session-state hash would be required for operational evidence.
Persistent trace storage would be required for operational evidence.
Append-only ledger semantics would be required for operational evidence.
Hash-chain verification would be required for operational evidence.
Cached artifact storage reference would be required for operational evidence.
Backend validation would be required for operational evidence.
Authenticated actor identity would be required for operational evidence.
Storage/audit retention model would be required for operational evidence.

assertions.expected_boundary_note: Illustrative cache-hit semantics only; no persisted artifact retrieval.
